methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Senra Systems is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $25M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of nine comparable companies—one public and eight private—operating in the same sector and stage cohort, including 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and Fairmat.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a standard 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to subsequent stage, anchoring to the $25M total raised as a proxy base value, yielding $50M. Peer-median valuation uses a cohort of six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $1.1B and a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. This approach reflects the significant dispersion typical of early-stage robotics, where outcomes vary materially by technology maturity and market traction.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated range of $42M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $562M. The wide dispersion reflects genuine uncertainty in the peer set—particularly the presence of 1X Technologies at $10.0B relative to the $300M–$500M band occupied by earlier peers. Key uncertainties include how Senra's unit economics and customer pipeline compare to the Series B cohort, whether capital efficiency to date exceeds peer norms, and the company's position relative to the sector's technical and commercialization milestones. The stage-progression method suggests a floor around $42M–$50M, but peer multiples imply considerably higher value conditional on execution risk assumptions.
